Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for Dr. Anand Mehta and Canopy Health correspondence using search_emails (query: "Mehta" or "Anand Mehta" or "Canopy" or similar). The agent needs to find Patricia Langford's original request to Mina Hashimoto for the executive relocation (Boston to Chicago, May 5 start), Fatimah's welcome email to Dr. Mehta outlining the full relocation process, Fatimah's housing request email to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest asking about furnished options in River North and Gold Coast, and Dr. Mehta's reply with his equipment inventory (two calibrated Eizo RadiForce RX660 monitors worth approximately $11,800 each with serial numbers RX660-1147A and RX660-1203A, and a SentrySafe DH-109E filing cabinet weighing approximately 350 lbs containing 14 binders of patient research data requiring chain-of-custody documentation). The agent should also discover that Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest did respond with three furnished housing options around April 21-22, with 48-hour holds that have since expired.
Search Slack for Dr. Mehta and Canopy executive relocation discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mehta" or "executive relocation" or "white-glove" or "Canopy CMO" or similar, filter_in_channel: #operations or #customer-engagement). The agent needs to discover Chloe Vance assigning Fatimah as coordinator, discussions about the three separate workstreams (temporary housing, medical equipment chain-of-custody, household move), David Chen's note about the strategic retention signal for the Canopy relationship, and any updates from Mina about the CRM deal.
Search Linear for Dr. Mehta's workstream tracking tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Mehta" or "Canopy" or "housing" or "chain-of-custody" or similar). The agent needs to find the three Linear issues Fatimah created to track each workstream separately: linear_issue_bd446671ceed for temporary housing, linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a for the medical equipment with chain-of-custody requirements, and linear_issue_4902f5c68acb for the main household move coordination. The agent should note the current status of each ticket and whether they have been updated recently.
Search Airtable for Dr. Mehta's relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Anand Mehta" or similar) or airtable_list_records. The agent should discover that no Airtable record exists yet for Dr. Mehta, meaning one will need to be created during the write-action phase. The expected record details include Company: Canopy Health, Origin: Boston MA, Destination: Chicago IL, white-glove service, pregnant partner considerations, calibrated lab equipment with chain-of-custody, and 3 to 4 weeks temporary corporate housing.
Search CRM for Canopy Health deals and engagements related to the Mehta relocation using crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy") or crm_search_deals (dealname: "Mehta" or "Canopy" or similar) and crm_list_engagements. The agent needs to find the executive relocation deal (crm_deal_c7ed21dc7fdb, $12,170) and any engagement notes documenting the coordination between Mina, Chloe, and Fatimah.
Search emails for Dr. Renata Kovacs and the Nashville to Toronto cross-border relocation using search_emails (query: "Kovacs" or "Nashville" or "Toronto" or "TN visa" or "BorderEase" or similar). The agent needs to find correspondence about the international move, visa documentation coordination, and the BorderEase Customs engagement for the Canadian customs side. The agent should discover that the Kovacs move has been completed (around April 14-17) with zero reported issues, that BorderEase Customs was formally engaged with an $850 surcharge on invoice INV-2026-0401, and that the personal effects declaration (BSF186) was filed successfully.
Search Slack for Kovacs cross-border relocation discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Kovacs" or "Toronto" or "visa" or "customs" or "BorderEase" or similar, filter_in_channel: #operations). The agent needs to find Fatimah's flag about visa and customs complexity in #operations and discussions between Fatimah and Chloe about using BorderEase Customs for the Canadian side. Combined with the Airtable move dates (April 14-17, now past) and the absence of any reported issues in email or Slack, the agent should infer that the Kovacs move completed successfully. The agent should also discover the $850 surcharge that may still be pending explicit client approval from Patricia Langford.
Search Airtable for Dr. Kovacs relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Kovacs" or similar). The agent needs to find the record (recKovacsNashvilleToronto) showing Company: Canopy Health, Origin: Nashville TN, Destination: Toronto Canada, Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, with special requirements including TN visa documentation, Canadian customs broker coordination, and personal effects declaration. The agent should note the current status and update it to Completed based on what the investigation reveals.
Search emails for Noah Fitzpatrick and Axiom Precision correspondence using search_emails (query: "Noah Fitzpatrick" or "Axiom" or "Axiom Precision" or similar). The agent needs to find Emeka Diallo's handoff of the Axiom intake to Fatimah, and the confirmation email Fatimah sent to Noah Fitzpatrick (or Carla Mendenhall at Axiom) with the relocation package details for the Seattle move. The agent should examine the confirmation details carefully, including the apartment address, flight confirmation number, and shipping information.
Search emails for Noah Fitzgerald and GreenStack Energy correspondence using search_emails (query: "Noah Fitzgerald" or "GreenStack" or similar). The agent needs to find the confirmed relocation package for Noah Fitzgerald going to Seattle, including apartment details, flight booking, and shipping arrangements confirmed for Noah Fitzgerald by MoveOps (Fatimah coordinated via UrbanNest for housing and Atlas Corporate Travel for flights). The agent should compare these details against what was sent to Axiom's Noah Fitzpatrick and notice that the apartment (412 Boylston Ave E, Apt 3B, Capitol Hill, Seattle) and the flight (Alaska Airlines AS 422, confirmation ACT-77492, April 14) appear in both employees' records.
Search CRM for both Axiom Precision and GreenStack Energy using crm_search_companies (name: "Axiom") and crm_search_companies (name: "GreenStack" or similar). The agent needs to find deal and engagement records for both companies. The agent should discover that the relocation details in the Axiom CRM records (apartment address, flight confirmation, shipping schedule) match the GreenStack Noah Fitzgerald confirmation rather than being unique to Noah Fitzpatrick. This is the critical mix-up: Fatimah was managing both Noah relocations simultaneously (same first name, same origin Philadelphia, same destination Seattle, overlapping timeframe) and pulled the wrong file's details into the other employee's confirmation package.
Search Airtable for both Noah relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Noah Fitzgerald") and separately (value: "Noah Fitzpatrick"). The agent needs to find that a record exists for Noah Fitzgerald (recReloc00000006) but no record exists for Noah Fitzpatrick. The agent should compare the Fitzgerald record's apartment, flight, and shipping details against what was sent to Fitzpatrick at Axiom and confirm that the same details (412 Boylston Ave E, Apt 3B and flight ACT-77492) appear in both employees' packages.
Search Slack for references to Noah Fitzpatrick, Axiom, or the Axiom intake using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Noah Fitzpatrick" or "Axiom" or "Fitzpatrick" or similar). The agent needs to find any coordination messages about the Axiom move and check whether the confirmation details discussed in Slack match GreenStack's Noah Fitzgerald rather than being specific to Axiom's Noah Fitzpatrick.
Search Linear for any issues related to the Axiom or GreenStack Noah relocations using linear_list_issues (query: "Noah" or "Axiom" or "Fitzpatrick" or "Fitzgerald" or similar). The agent needs to check whether there are tracking tickets for either Noah's relocation and whether the details in those tickets reflect the same mix-up found in email and CRM.
Search emails and Slack for information about recent chain-of-custody failures on other relocations using search_emails (query: "chain-of-custody" or "chain of custody" or "security escort" or "NorthWind" or "Victor Huang" or "Mosaic" or "prototype" or similar) and conversations_search_messages (search_query: "chain-of-custody" or "custody" or "escort" or "NorthWind" or "Mosaic" or similar). The agent needs to discover two relevant incidents. First, the NorthWind Technologies incident involving Raj Patel's server lab relocation (Chicago to Denver) where the MoveOps crew arrived and started loading equipment before the required IT security escort was on site, and the equipment was then placed in overnight warehouse storage in direct violation of the no-warehouse-storage requirement. Victor Huang, NorthWind's CISO, is treating it as a formal security breach. Second, the Mosaic Robotics incident involving Zara Okoye's relocation (Savannah to Atlanta) where a $90,000 robotic arm prototype was damaged because coordinator Blessing Okafor signed off on the packing checklist remotely without physically verifying the anti-vibration foam crating spec, and Heartland Movers made an unscheduled overnight stop at their Macon depot. The damage resulted in a $40,000 insurance coverage gap for MoveOps. The agent should connect both failures to Dr. Mehta's relocation since Mehta also has equipment requiring chain-of-custody handling (calibrated lab monitors and a secured filing cabinet), and flag specific safeguards Fatimah should implement to prevent the same kinds of breakdowns.
Search Linear for NorthWind or Mosaic chain-of-custody related tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "chain-of-custody" or "custody" or "breach" or "Mosaic" or similar). The agent needs to find tracking issues related to either incident and check what remediation steps or process changes were documented that could inform Dr. Mehta's equipment handling protocol.
Look up Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Carmen Reyes" or "UrbanNest" or similar). The agent needs to find carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com in order to send the housing follow-up email.
Look up the #operations Slack channel using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel") to retrieve the channel ID (C006) needed for posting the status summary.
Create an Airtable record for Dr. Mehta and update records for Kovacs and the Noah situation using airtable_create_record (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations") and airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01). For Dr. Mehta: create a new record with fields including Name, Company (Canopy Health), Origin (Boston), Destination (Chicago), Status (In Progress), Move Start Date, Assigned Coordinator (Fatimah Al-Rashidi), and Special Requirements covering the three workstreams, equipment inventory, housing status, and chain-of-custody notes informed by the NorthWind and Mosaic incidents. For Dr. Kovacs: update record recKovacsNashvilleToronto to set Status to Completed and update Special Requirements with the completed move details including BorderEase engagement, BSF186 filing, and the $850 surcharge. For the Noah situation: the agent should create a new Airtable record for Noah Fitzpatrick at Axiom Precision since no record currently exists, documenting the relocation details and flagging the data cross-contamination with Noah Fitzgerald's GreenStack package (same apartment 412 Boylston Ave E and same flight ACT-77492). The agent should also update Noah Fitzgerald's existing record (recReloc00000006) to flag the same mix-up from his side.
Update or add comments to the existing Linear tickets for Dr. Mehta's three workstreams using linear_create_comment. For the housing ticket (linear_issue_bd446671ceed): add a comment noting that Carmen at UrbanNest responded with three options (River North at approximately $1,250/week, Streeterville at approximately $1,350/week, Gold Coast at approximately $1,450/week), that the 48-hour holds have expired, that Dr. Mehta has not confirmed his selection, and that a follow-up email has been sent to Carmen. For the equipment chain-of-custody ticket (linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a): add a comment with the full equipment inventory received from Dr. Mehta, reference both the NorthWind and Mosaic chain-of-custody failures as lessons learned, and document mandatory safeguards such as in-person coordinator presence for packing, no remote sign-offs on items over $10K, direct transport only with no warehouse or depot stops, and authorized recipient at delivery. For the main household move ticket (linear_issue_4902f5c68acb): add a comment noting that the move is pending and blocked on housing confirmation for the Chicago delivery address, with Swift Relocations as the planned vendor and pack dates around April 28-30.
Send a follow-up email to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest about Dr. Mehta's temporary housing using send_email (sender: fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, recipients: carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com, subject related to Dr. Mehta's furnished housing for Canopy Health). The content should request an update on whether the three units previously discussed are still available now that the 48-hour holds have expired, express priority for the Streeterville option (The Pinnacle), note the May 5 start date and the need for 3 to 4 weeks of temporary corporate housing, and ask about comparable replacement options if any units have been taken.
Post a comprehensive status summary in the #operations Slack channel (C006) using conversations_add_message. The message should cover all three situations so that Chloe has full visibility on Fatimah's workload: (1) Dr. Mehta status including housing options received from Carmen, expired holds, equipment inventory confirmed, chain-of-custody preparation with safeguards drawn from both the NorthWind and Mosaic incidents, and current blockers (housing confirmation overdue, chain-of-custody template needed from Patricia Langford); (2) Dr. Kovacs status confirmed as completed with BorderEase engaged, customs documentation filed, and the $850 surcharge still potentially pending client approval; (3) the Noah Fitzpatrick and Fitzgerald cross-contamination showing that both employees received the same apartment (412 Boylston Ave E, Apt 3B) and same flight (ACT-77492) due to Fatimah managing two identical-profile relocations simultaneously, along with what corrective steps are needed; and (4) any immediate action items or blockers that need Chloe's attention before the May push.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

The agent needs to discover two relevant incidents. First, the NorthWind Technologies incident involving Raj Patel's server lab relocation (Chicago to Denver) where the MoveOps crew arrived and started loading equipment before the required IT security escort was on site, and the equipment was then placed in overnight warehouse storage in direct violation of the no-warehouse-storage requirement. Victor Huang, NorthWind's CISO, is treating it as a formal security breach. Second, the Mosaic Robotics incident involving Zara Okoye's relocation (Savannah to Atlanta) where a $90,000 robotic arm prototype was damaged because coordinator Blessing Okafor signed off on the packing checklist remotely without physically verifying the anti-vibration foam crating spec, and Heartland Movers made an unscheduled overnight stop at their Macon depot. The damage resulted in a $40,000 insurance coverage gap for MoveOps. The agent should connect both failures to Dr. Mehta's relocation since Mehta also has equipment requiring chain-of-custody handling (calibrated lab monitors and a secured filing cabinet), and flag specific safeguards Fatimah should implement to prevent the same kinds of breakdowns.
Search Linear for NorthWind or Mosaic chain-of-custody related tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "chain-of-custody" or "custody" or "breach" or "Mosaic" or similar). The agent needs to find tracking issues related to either incident and check what remediation steps or process changes were documented that could inform Dr. Mehta's equipment handling protocol.
Look up Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Carmen Reyes" or "UrbanNest" or similar). The agent needs to find carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com in order to send the housing follow-up email.
Look up the #operations Slack channel using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel") to retrieve the channel ID (C006) needed for posting the status summary.
Create an Airtable record for Dr. Mehta and update records for Kovacs and the Noah situation using airtable_create_record (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations") and airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01). For Dr. Mehta: create a new record with fields including Name, Company (Canopy Health), Origin (Boston), Destination (Chicago), Status (In Progress), Move Start Date, Assigned Coordinator (Fatimah Al-Rashidi), and Special Requirements covering the three workstreams, equipment inventory, housing status, and chain-of-custody notes informed by the NorthWind and Mosaic incidents. For Dr. Kovacs: update record recKovacsNashvilleToronto to set Status to Completed and update Special Requirements with the completed move details including BorderEase engagement, BSF186 filing, and the $850 surcharge. For the Noah situation: the agent should create a new Airtable record for Noah Fitzpatrick at Axiom Precision since no record currently exists, documenting the relocation details and flagging the data cross-contamination with Noah Fitzgerald's GreenStack package (same apartment 412 Boylston Ave E and same flight ACT-77492). The agent should also update Noah Fitzgerald's existing record (recReloc00000006) to flag the same mix-up from his side.
Update or add comments to the existing Linear tickets for Dr. Mehta's three workstreams using linear_create_comment. For the housing ticket (linear_issue_bd446671ceed): add a comment noting that Carmen at UrbanNest responded with three options (River North at approximately $1,250/week, Streeterville at approximately $1,350/week, Gold Coast at approximately $1,450/week), that the 48-hour holds have expired, that Dr. Mehta has not confirmed his selection, and that a follow-up email has been sent to Carmen. For the equipment chain-of-custody ticket (linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a): add a comment with the full equipment inventory received from Dr. Mehta, reference both the NorthWind and Mosaic chain-of-custody failures as lessons learned, and document mandatory safeguards such as in-person coordinator presence for packing, no remote sign-offs on items over $10K, direct transport only with no warehouse or depot stops, and authorized recipient at delivery. For the main household move ticket (linear_issue_4902f5c68acb): add a comment noting that the move is pending and blocked on housing confirmation for the Chicago delivery address, with Swift Relocations as the planned vendor and pack dates around April 28-30.
Send a follow-up email to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest about Dr. Mehta's temporary housing using send_email (sender: fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, recipients: carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com, subject related to Dr. Mehta's furnished housing for Canopy Health). The content should request an update on whether the three units previously discussed are still available now that the 48-hour holds have expired, express priority for the Streeterville option (The Pinnacle), note the May 5 start date and the need for 3 to 4 weeks of temporary corporate housing, and ask about comparable replacement options if any units have been taken.
Post a comprehensive status summary in the #operations Slack channel (C006) using conversations_add_message. The message should cover all three situations so that Chloe has full visibility on Fatimah's workload: (1) Dr. Mehta status including housing options received from Carmen, expired holds, equipment inventory confirmed, chain-of-custody preparation with safeguards drawn from both the NorthWind and Mosaic incidents, and current blockers (housing confirmation overdue, chain-of-custody template needed from Patricia Langford); (2) Dr. Kovacs status confirmed as completed with BorderEase engaged, customs documentation filed, and the $850 surcharge still potentially pending client approval; (3) the Noah Fitzpatrick and Fitzgerald cross-contamination showing that both employees received the same apartment (412 Boylston Ave E, Apt 3B) and same flight (ACT-77492) due to Fatimah managing two identical-profile relocations simultaneously, along with what corrective steps are needed; and (4) any immediate action items or blockers that need Chloe's attention before the May push.
